action.reasoning:
Observation: Data confirmed - sequences ~735 AA, mutations in positions 560-587 (28 positions, indels possible since lengths vary 734-737). Targets are continuous, range ~-10 to 10. Decision: Build baseline using ESM2-35M frozen, extract embeddings for the mutation region (positions ~555-595 with some context), mean pool + MLP head. Pre-compute embeddings to speed up training. Actually, since lengths vary (insertions/deletions), I should pass the full mutation region. Let me extract positions 555:600 (with context), pad/truncate to fixed length, run through ESM2-35M frozen, and train regression head. Start simple: pre-compute ESM embeddings once, train MLP fast.

Let me try a different approach: just use ESM2-35M directly on the variable region only (which is short, ~30-35 AA), so encoding is fast. Pre-compute embeddings for all 28K + 3K + 50K test sequences. Then MLP regression.
